flashcards.front: What is Infrastructure as a Service (IaaS)?
flashcards.back: IaaS is a cloud computing service model where virtualized computing resources (servers, storage, networking) are provided over the internet. Users manage OS, middleware, and applications while the cloud provider maintains the physical infrastructure.

flashcards.front: What is Platform as a Service (PaaS)?
flashcards.back: PaaS provides a development and deployment environment in the cloud, including operating systems, programming languages, and tools. Developers focus on application code while the cloud provider manages infrastructure, runtime, and middleware.

flashcards.front: What is Software as a Service (SaaS)?
flashcards.back: SaaS delivers fully functional software applications over the internet on a subscription basis. The cloud provider manages everything—from infrastructure to applications—while users access the software via web browsers or APIs.

flashcards.front: Draw the Shared Responsibility Model for IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS.
flashcards.back: IaaS: Provider manages physical infrastructure, virtualization, network, storage; User manages OS, middleware, runtime, data, applications. PaaS: Provider adds OS, middleware, runtime; User manages applications and data. SaaS: Provider manages everything; User manages user access and data.

flashcards.front: Compare IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS on scalability, control, and management burden.
flashcards.back: IaaS offers maximum control and scalability but high management burden. PaaS provides moderate control with balanced scalability and management. SaaS offers least control and minimal management but automatic scaling. Cost increases from IaaS to SaaS.

flashcards.front: A startup wants to quickly deploy a mobile app without managing servers. Which cloud model and why?
flashcards.back: PaaS is ideal—provides built-in development frameworks, databases, and auto-scaling. Examples: Heroku, Google App Engine, AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Reduces time-to-market as the startup focuses on coding rather than infrastructure provisioning.

flashcards.front: An enterprise needs to migrate legacy databases with custom configurations. Which IaaS component is essential?
flashcards.back: Virtual Machines (VMs) are essential—IaaS provides customizable virtual servers to run legacy operating systems and databases. Services like AWS EC2, Azure Virtual Machines, or Google Compute Engine allow full control over the database environment.

flashcards.front: Explain why an organization might use a hybrid cloud approach combining IaaS and SaaS.
flashcards.back: Organizations use SaaS for standard functions (email, CRM like Salesforce) while using IaaS for sensitive workloads requiring custom infrastructure (private databases, legacy apps). This optimizes cost, maintains compliance, and provides flexibility.
